message:
Show actor on status/pinned change events in activity feed

Added actor field to RPC client and set it before daemon requests.
This ensures status changes (like pinned events) show who performed
the action in bd activity output.

Changes:
- Added actor field to Client struct
- Added SetActor method to set actor for audit trail
- Modified ExecuteWithCwd to include actor in RPC requests
- Updated main.go to call SetActor after daemon connection

Fixes gt-1ydd9

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-7yg","title":"Git merge driver uses invalid placeholders (%L, %R instead of %A, %B)","description":"## Problem

The beads git merge driver is configured with invalid Git placeholders:

```
git config merge.beads.driver "bd merge %A %O %L %R"
```

Git doesn't recognize `%L` or `%R` as valid merge driver placeholders. The valid placeholders are:
- `%O` = base (common ancestor)
- `%A` = current version (ours)
- `%B` = other version (theirs)

## Impact

- Affects ALL users when they have `.beads/beads.jsonl` merge conflicts
- Automatic JSONL merge fails with error: "error reading left file: failed to open file: open 7: no such file or directory"
- Users must manually resolve conflicts instead of getting automatic merge

## Root Cause

The `bd init` command (or wherever the merge driver is configured) is using non-standard placeholders. When Git encounters `%L` and `%R`, it either passes them literally or interprets them incorrectly.

## Fix

Update the merge driver configuration to:
```
git config merge.beads.driver "bd merge %A %O %A %B"
```

Where:
- 1st `%A` = output file (current file, will be overwritten)
- `%O` = base (common ancestor)
- 2nd `%A` = left/current version
- `%B` = right/other version

## Action Items

1. Fix `bd init` (or equivalent setup command) to use correct placeholders
2. Add migration/warning for existing users with misconfigured merge driver
3. Update documentation with correct merge driver setup
4. Consider adding validation when `bd init` is run","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-21T19:51:55.747608-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}

 {"id":"bd-abjw","title":"Consider consolidating config.yaml parsing into shared utility","description":"Multiple places parse config.yaml with custom structs:

1. **autoimport.go:148** - `localConfig{SyncBranch}`
2. **main.go:310** - strings.Contains for no-db (fragile, see bd-r6k2)
3. **doctor.go:863** - strings.Contains for no-db (fragile, see bd-r6k2)
4. **internal/config/config.go** - Uses viper (but caches at startup, problematic for tests)

Consider creating a shared utility in `internal/configfile/` or extending the viper config:

```go
// internal/configfile/yaml.go
type YAMLConfig struct {
    SyncBranch string `yaml:"sync-branch"`
    NoDb       bool   `yaml:"no-db"`
    IssuePrefix string `yaml:"issue-prefix"`
    Author     string `yaml:"author"`
}

func LoadYAML(beadsDir string) (*YAMLConfig, error) {
    // Parse config.yaml with proper YAML library
}
```

Benefits:
- Single source of truth for config.yaml structure
- Proper YAML parsing everywhere
- Easier to add new config fields

Trade-off: May add complexity for simple one-off reads.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T02:03:26.067311-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T02:03:26.067311-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-hobo","title":"Distinct prefixes for protos, molecules, wisps","description":"Template/workflow entities should have visually distinct prefixes from regular issues.

**Problem:**
- Protos (bd-7bs4) look like regular issues - invites squashing
- Molecules (poured instances) also use bd- prefix
- Wisps are in separate DB but still use bd- when referenced

**Proposed Prefixes:**
- `proto-` for templates (e.g., proto-release, proto-review)
- `mol-` for active molecules (poured from protos)
- `wisp-` for ephemeral wisps (vapor phase)

**Benefits:**
- Instant visual recognition of entity type
- Prevents accidental modification of templates
- Clear lifecycle: proto → mol → wisp → digest

**Implementation options:**
1. Separate databases with different prefixes
2. Issue type determines prefix generation
3. Naming convention enforced by bd pour/wisp commands","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-24T16:45:24.940809-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T02:04:52.459233-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T02:04:52.459233-08:00","labels":["workflow"]}

 {"id":"bd-xo1o.2","title":"WaitsFor directive: Fanout gate for dynamic children","description":"Implement WaitsFor directive for molecules that spawn dynamic children.

## Syntax
```markdown
## Step: aggregate
Collect outcomes from all dynamically-bonded children.
WaitsFor: all-children
Needs: survey-workers
```

## Behavior
1. Parse WaitsFor directive during molecule step parsing
2. Track which steps spawn dynamic children (the spawner)
3. Gate step waits until ALL children of the spawner complete
4. Works with bd ready - gate step not ready until children done

## Gate Types
- `WaitsFor: all-children` - Wait for all dynamic children
- `WaitsFor: any-children` - Proceed when first child completes (future)
- `WaitsFor: <step-ref>.children` - Wait for specific spawner's children

## Integration
- bd ready should skip gate steps until children complete
- bd show <mol> should display gate status and child count","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T02:33:14.946475-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T04:00:09.443106-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T04:00:09.443106-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xo1o.2","depends_on_id":"bd-xo1o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T02:33:14.950008-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-xo1o.3","title":"bd activity: Real-time molecule state feed","description":"Implement activity feed command for watching molecule state transitions.

## Commands
```bash
bd activity --follow              # Real-time streaming
bd activity --mol <id>            # Activity for specific molecule
bd activity --since 5m            # Last 5 minutes
bd activity --type step           # Only step transitions
```

## Output Format
```
[14:32:01] ✓ patrol-x7k.inbox-check completed
[14:32:03] ✓ patrol-x7k.check-refinery completed
[14:32:08] + patrol-x7k.arm-ace bonded (5 steps)
[14:32:09] → patrol-x7k.arm-ace.capture in_progress
[14:32:10] ✓ patrol-x7k.arm-ace.capture completed
[14:32:14] ✓ patrol-x7k.arm-ace.decide completed (action: nudge-1)
[14:32:17] ✓ patrol-x7k.arm-ace COMPLETE
[14:32:23] ✓ patrol-x7k SQUASHED → digest-x7k
```

## Event Types
- `+` bonded - New molecule/step created
- `→` in_progress - Step started
- `✓` completed - Step/molecule finished
- `✗` failed - Step failed
- `⊘` burned - Wisp discarded
- `◉` squashed - Wisp condensed to digest

## Implementation
- Could use SQLite triggers or polling
- --follow uses OS file watching or polling
- Filter by mol ID, type, time range","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T02:33:16.298764-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T03:18:33.434079-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T03:18:33.434079-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xo1o.3","depends_on_id":"bd-xo1o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T02:33:16.301522-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-xo1o.4","title":"Parallel step detection in molecules","description":"Detect and flag parallelizable steps in molecules.

## Detection Rules
Steps can run in parallel when:
1. No Needs dependencies between them
2. Not in same sequential chain
3. Across dynamic arms (arm-ace and arm-nux can parallelize)

## Output in bd mol show
```
patrol-x7k (mol-witness-patrol)
├── inbox-check [completed]
├── survey-workers [completed]
│   ├── arm-ace [parallel group A]
│   │   ├── capture [can parallelize]
│   │   ├── assess [needs: capture]
│   │   └── execute [needs: assess]
│   └── arm-nux [parallel group A]
│       ├── capture [can parallelize]
│       └── ...
├── aggregate [gate: waits for all-children]
```

## Flags
- `bd mol show <id> --parallel` - Highlight parallel opportunities
- `bd ready --mol <id>` - List all steps that can run now

## Future: Parallel Execution Hints
For agents using Task tool subagents:
- Identify independent arms that can run simultaneously
- Suggest parallelization strategy","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T02:33:17.660368-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T03:56:39.653982-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T03:56:39.653982-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xo1o.4","depends_on_id":"bd-xo1o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T02:33:17.662232-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-xrwy","title":"Auto-detect non-TTY and adjust output","description":"Detect when stdout is not a TTY (piped, redirected, or agent context) and adjust output:

- Disable ANSI colors
- Disable pager
- Disable emoji decorations
- Use plain text format

Uses golang.org/x/term or similar for TTY detection.

Needs pager support to be meaningful (otherwise nothing to disable).","notes":"## Implementation Plan

### Dependencies
```go
import "golang.org/x/term"
// or: github.com/mattn/go-isatty
```

### Code Changes

1. **Add TTY detection helper** (internal/ui/terminal.go):
```go
func IsTerminal() bool {
    return term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd()))
}

func ShouldUseColor() bool {
    // Respect NO_COLOR standard
    if os.Getenv("NO_COLOR") \!= "" {
        return false
    }
    // Respect CLICOLOR=0
    if os.Getenv("CLICOLOR") == "0" {
        return false
    }
    // Force color with CLICOLOR_FORCE
    if os.Getenv("CLICOLOR_FORCE") \!= "" {
        return true
    }
    return IsTerminal()
}
```

2. **Apply in lipgloss setup** (internal/ui/styles.go):
```go
func init() {
    if \!ShouldUseColor() {
        lipgloss.SetColorProfile(termenv.Ascii)
    }
}
```

3. **Disable pager for non-TTY** (uses bd-jdz3 infrastructure):
```go
// In pager.go ToPager():
if \!IsTerminal() {
    fmt.Print(content)
    return nil
}
```

### Standards Followed
- NO_COLOR: https://no-color.org/
- CLICOLOR/CLICOLOR_FORCE: de facto BSD standard
- gh cli uses same conventions

### Testing
- `bd list | cat` outputs no ANSI codes
- `NO_COLOR=1 bd list` outputs no colors
- `CLICOLOR_FORCE=1 bd list | cat` keeps colors
- Verify emoji handling in non-TTY mode","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T15:25:10.112313-08:00","created_by":"stevey","updated_at":"2025-12-29T15:26:50.288486-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xrwy","depends_on_id":"bd-jdz3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T15:25:22.084746-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"bd-xrwy","title":"Auto-detect non-TTY and adjust output","description":"Detect when stdout is not a TTY (piped, redirected, or agent context) and adjust output:

- Disable ANSI colors
- Disable pager
- Disable emoji decorations
- Use plain text format

Uses golang.org/x/term or similar for TTY detection.

Needs pager support to be meaningful (otherwise nothing to disable).","notes":"## Implementation Plan

### Dependencies
```go
import "golang.org/x/term"
// or: github.com/mattn/go-isatty
```

### Code Changes

1. **Add TTY detection helper** (internal/ui/terminal.go):
```go
func IsTerminal() bool {
    return term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd()))
}

func ShouldUseColor() bool {
    // Respect NO_COLOR standard
    if os.Getenv("NO_COLOR") \!= "" {
        return false
    }
    // Respect CLICOLOR=0
    if os.Getenv("CLICOLOR") == "0" {
        return false
    }
    // Force color with CLICOLOR_FORCE
    if os.Getenv("CLICOLOR_FORCE") \!= "" {
        return true
    }
    return IsTerminal()
}
```

2. **Apply in lipgloss setup** (internal/ui/styles.go):
```go
func init() {
    if \!ShouldUseColor() {
        lipgloss.SetColorProfile(termenv.Ascii)
    }
}
```

3. **Disable pager for non-TTY** (uses bd-jdz3 infrastructure):
```go
// In pager.go ToPager():
if \!IsTerminal() {
    fmt.Print(content)
    return nil
}
```

### Standards Followed
- NO_COLOR: https://no-color.org/
- CLICOLOR/CLICOLOR_FORCE: de facto BSD standard
- gh cli uses same conventions

### Testing
- `bd list | cat` outputs no ANSI codes
- `NO_COLOR=1 bd list` outputs no colors
- `CLICOLOR_FORCE=1 bd list | cat` keeps colors
- Verify emoji handling in non-TTY mode","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"beads/polecats/onyx","created_at":"2025-12-29T15:25:10.112313-08:00","created_by":"stevey","updated_at":"2025-12-30T06:53:28.197853-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xrwy","depends_on_id":"bd-jdz3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T15:25:22.084746-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-xsl9","title":"Remove legacy autoflush code paths","description":"## Problem

The autoflush system has dual code paths - an old timer-based approach and a new FlushManager. Both are actively used based on whether flushManager is nil.

## Locations

- main.go:78-81: isDirty, needsFullExport, flushTimer marked 'used by legacy code'
- autoflush.go:291-369: Functions with 'Legacy path for backward compatibility with tests'

## Current Behavior

```go
// In markDirtyAndScheduleFlush():
if flushManager != nil {
    flushManager.MarkDirty(false)
    return
}
// Legacy path for backward compatibility with tests
```

## Proposed Fix

1. Ensure flushManager is always initialized (even in tests)
2. Remove the legacy timer-based code paths
3. Remove isDirty, needsFullExport, flushTimer globals
4. Update tests to use FlushManager

## Risk

Low - the FlushManager is the production path. Legacy code only runs when flushManager is nil (test scenarios).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T15:49:30.83769-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T01:54:59.09333-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T01:54:59.09333-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-xtf5","title":"Code smell: Multiple CLI command files exceed 1000 lines","description":"Several CLI command files are very large and could benefit from splitting:

| File | Lines | Notes |

| show.go | 1592 | Display formatting, tree views, output modes |
| doctor.go | 1295 | Many health checks |
| sync.go | 1201 | Sync branch operations |
| compact.go | 1199 | Compaction logic |
| linear.go | 1190 | Linear integration |
| main.go | 1148 | Entry point and globals |

Consider:
1. Splitting init.go into init_core.go, init_contributor.go (already exists), init_team.go (already exists)
2. Moving show.go formatters to internal/ui package
3. Doctor checks could be individual files under doctor/ subpackage (already started)

 {"id":"bd-ykqu","title":"Add gate timeout tracking and notification","description":"Implement timeout and notification logic for gates.

## Timeout Behavior
1. Gate created with timeout (e.g., 30m)
2. Deacon tracks elapsed time during patrol
3. If timeout reached:
   - Notify all waiters: "Gate timed out"
   - Close gate with timeout reason
   - Waiter can retry, escalate, or fail gracefully

## Notification
- Use gt mail send to notify waiters
- Include gate ID, await type, and reason in message
- Support multiple waiters notification

## Escalation Path
- Witness sees stuck worker, nudges them
- Worker can escalate to human if needed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:40.1825-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:44.362527-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:44.362527-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ykqu","depends_on_id":"bd-udsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:53.072862-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-ykqu","depends_on_id":"bd-is6m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:56.595085-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

-{"id":"bd-yuxq","title":"Code smell: 262 uses of interface{} reduce type safety","description":"Heavy use of interface{} across 92 files (262 occurrences) in:
- RPC update arguments: updatesFromArgs() returns map[string]interface{}
- JSON unmarshaling and formatting
- Storage query helpers

**Example in internal/rpc/server_issues_epics.go:45:**
```go
func updatesFromArgs(a UpdateArgs) map[string]interface{} {
    // Returns map with interface{} values - types must be asserted at use sites
}
```

**Problem:**
- Loss of type safety
- Runtime type assertions required
- Harder to catch errors at compile time

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Define typed update structs for specific field groups
- [ ] Use generics where possible (Go 1.18+)
- [ ] Reduce interface{} count by at least 50%
- [ ] Document expected types where interface{} remains necessary
- [ ] Tests pass","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:00:01.896147-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:42:12.539958-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yuxq","depends_on_id":"bd-ox1o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:00:23.812412-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

- [ ] Document expected types where interface{} remains necessary
